reasoning: The current reference price for April 10, 2026 (the last trading day before April 12) is 35.11. UDR is declaring a $0.435 dividend per share with a record date of April 15, 2026 (which will serve as the ex-dividend date given T+1 settlement). This will mechanically drop the share price by ~1.2% midway through the first week, making it highly unlikely that the April 19 close price recovers above 35.11 unless an unexpected positive catalyst happens. Near-term guidance projects a mild decrease in FFOA against a backdrop of flat to negative recent trend, keeping near-term probabilies relatively low but increasing throughout the year as fundamental operations or rate expectations shift. Over the very long-term timeframe (3 to 10 years), even high-dividend REITs typically drift upward mechanically with broader inflation in asset prices, though slower than common market equities. Because UDR currently trades at ~$35 (well below cycle highs but above the 2015 ~$32 prints), long-term reversion offers a solid chance to breach above 35.11. Probabilities scale smoothly starting mostly bearish (due to structural ex-dividend handicap in week 1) upwards towards the S&P 500 baseline drift in a 10y window.
